name: Renamed PsExec Detected
description: Detection of a renamed PsExec binary. PsExec is a legitimate utility but is often abused by attackers for lateral movement and remote code execution. Adversaries may rename it to evade detection by security software. This detection looks for a known hash or file size of PsExec when executed under a different name.

prompts.prompt: Investigate the execution of the renamed PsExec binary on host `{hostname}` at `{timestamp}`. The original binary was PsExec, but it was executed as `{renamed_binary}`. The command line detected was `{command_line}`. Analyze the user (`{user_name}`) who executed the command and the destination host (`{destination_host}`). Determine if this is a legitimate administrative action or a malicious attempt at lateral movement. Provide a summary of the findings and suggest immediate containment and remediation steps.

parameters.name: hostname
parameters.description: The hostname where the renamed PsExec was executed.

parameters.name: renamed_binary
parameters.description: The new name of the PsExec binary.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the renamed binary.

parameters.name: user_name
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.

parameters.name: destination_host
parameters.description: The destination host where PsExec attempted to establish a session.
